w:r.w:t: Financial clarity for patients. Financial certainty for hospitals.
This guide supersedes both the hackathon blueprint and project brief v4. Where they conflict, this document wins. Both sides (patient + hospital), upgraded JSON dataset, Claude API.

An AI multi-agent system with two modes running on the same platform. Patient mode predicts out-of-pocket costs before a visit. Hospital mode predicts claim denial risk before submission — connecting the two sides of healthcare billing that have never been linked.
Patient enters: procedure, insurance plan, location, provider type, urgency, deductible status.
Hospital enters: clinical note, CPT code, ICD code, payer.
Gets: cost range, out-of-pocket, insurance split, prior auth flag, Claude explanation, savings tips.
Gets: denial risk score (0–95%), NCCI check, missing docs list, fix list with example wording.
Four AI agents orchestrated by LangGraph. All agents share a single state hash map — this is what makes the system a closed loop. Claude API powers all LLM calls.
Shared state, agent routing via Supervisor
Cost explanation, note analysis, fix list generation
Reads state mode flag. Routes to patient-side or hospital-side workers. ~20 lines.
Queries pricing_data.json + fee_schedule.json. Applies mocked benefits. Calls Claude for explanation.
Checks member_benefits.json for plan type. Verifies prior auth. Flags coverage gaps. Feeds both sides.
Runs NCCI edit check. Scores denial risk. Calls Claude to analyze clinical note. Generates fix list.
CRITICAL: Agree on these exact field names before anyone writes code. Mismatched keys break the shared state silently.
patient_input: { cpt_code, zip_code, insurance_type, provider_type, urgency, deductible_status }
cost_estimate: { total_low, total_high, oop_low, oop_high, insurance_low, insurance_high }
Claude replaces GPT-4o for all LLM calls. The LLM never generates a number. Every dollar and percentage traces to a JSON file.
"knee MRI" → CPT 73721. Text to structured fields.
Reads mode, picks path. Can be if/else for MVP.
Takes real numbers, writes plain-English cost breakdown + savings tips.
Reads clinical note, identifies missing documentation for medical necessity.
Generates specific fix actions with example wording per risk factor.
Stored in .env as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — never committed to repo
Streamlit entry: two tabs, forms, output display
LangGraph StateGraph: schema, nodes, routing edges
Reads mode, routes to cost or denial path
CMS lookup + benefits calc + Claude explanation
Benefits check, prior auth flag (shared by both)
NCCI check + risk scoring + Claude fix list
CMS-structured provider rates by CPT + state
Medicare rates + commercial multipliers by state
PTP edit pairs + MUE limits per CPT
Mocked insurance plans + prior auth rules
Mocked denial rates + risk factors + 6 scenarios
Claude prompt for note analysis + fix list
Exactly as listed in Section 3. Copy-paste, do not retype.
One person creates Anthropic account, shares via .env only
Plain text, no markdown. Each prompt specifies structure.
JSON file keys must match what agents expect. Validate before integration.
Three people, three clean ownership zones, fully parallel from hour 0. All write to the same shared LangGraph state — integration is one merge at hour 10.
Repo setup: venv, install langgraph + anthropic + streamlit. Define StateGraph with full TypedDict schema. Share schema with P2/P3 immediately.
Supervisor agent: reads mode, routes to cost_predictor or denial_predictor. Stub agents return hardcoded demo data. Test full graph: START → supervisor → stub → END.

Streamlit UI: two tabs. Patient tab: procedure, insurance, zip, provider, urgency, deductible. Hospital tab: note textarea, CPT, ICD, payer. Wire to graph. Build output components: cost card, split bar, denial badge, fix list.
UI polish: prior auth banner, savings tips, loading states, transparency footer. Pre-fill demo scenarios.
INTEGRATION: Replace stubs with P2/P3 real agents. Fix field mismatches. End-to-end test both tabs.
Demo prep: dry-run 3x, backup screenshots, finalize script.
Data loaders: load pricing_data.json + fee_schedule.json. Function: CPT + state → providers + Medicare rate. Map CPT to scenario key.
compute_estimate(): provider rates + commercial multiplier + benefits → { total_low, total_high, oop_low, oop_high, insurance_low, insurance_high }. Pure Python.
Coverage analyzer: load member_benefits.json, check prior auth for CPT+payer, calc deductible + coinsurance. Write to state.
Cost predictor agent: reads state, calls loader, calls compute, calls Claude for explanation. Write cost_prompt.py.
Test all 5 CPT scenarios. Validate numbers. Edge cases: unknown CPT, different plans.
INTEGRATION: Hand agents to P1. Fix state mismatches. Help with E2E testing.
NCCI loader: load ncci_edits.json. Build check_ptp_edits(primary, others) and check_mue(cpt, units). Test: 73721+73720 flags.
Risk scorer: load denial_rules.json. min(0.95, base_rate + sum(risk_increases)). Map status tiers.
Denial predictor agent: NCCI check → prior auth check → Claude note analysis → risk score. Write denial_prompt.py.
Fix list: actionable fixes from denial_rules.json + Claude wording. Test before/after: 34% → 4%.
Test all 6 demo scenarios. Validate scores. Prepare demo clinical note.
INTEGRATION: Hand agents to P1. Fix state mismatches. Help with E2E testing.
Hours 12–14 (all three): Final QA, demo rehearsal x3, backup screenshots, README.
9. Demo script — Knee MRI scenario (3 min)
"Healthcare is the only industry where you don't know the price until after you buy — and where providers don't know if they'll get paid until months later. We fix both sides. Let us show you."
Knee MRI, Aetna PPO, zip 06604. Hit estimate.
"$350–$520 out-of-pocket. From real CMS data structures."
"Facility fees, outpatient vs hospital. 15–30% savings at imaging centers."
"Flagged before booking: Aetna requires auth for this procedure."
Switch PPO to HMO. Show OOP changes instantly.
CPT 73721, ICD M17.11, Aetna PPO. Submit.
"Code combo valid — runs against real CMS coding rules."
"34% risk. Missing prior auth drives most of it."
Click fix. Score drops 34% → 4%. "No surprise bill. Both sides win."

"Our cost predictor and NCCI coding validator use real CMS data structures with representative values. Insurance benefits are simulated. The mock layers are clearly separated and swappable — it is one config change."
Can the AI hallucinate a price?

"No. The LLM never generates a number. Every dollar and denial percentage traces to a deterministic lookup against our data files. The LLM only parses input, routes decisions, and writes explanations around real numbers."

"Patient cost tools and denial prediction tools exist separately. Nobody has connected them into a single closed-loop system — because it requires understanding both sides of the transaction simultaneously."
What would it take to go to production?

"One key integration: Availity API for real-time benefits verification. The pricing layer already uses CMS-structured data. The LangGraph architecture is designed so each mock layer is a single swappable module."

"Claude excels at careful, structured reasoning on clinical text — exactly what denial risk analysis and documentation gap detection require. Claude also follows complex structured output instructions more reliably, which matters when agents depend on parseable responses from each other."
11. How to use this document with Claude
At the start of each work session, upload this PDF and use the prompt below. This gives Claude full project context so every team member gets consistent, aligned help.
"I am working on a hackathon project. This PDF is our unified build guide. Read it carefully. I am Person [1/2/3]. My current task is: [task from Section 7]. Help me build this step by step."
